reasoning: The FRED forecast tool provides a combined baseline probability of 0.51, which serves as my primary anchor. However, recent data points reveal a very clear rolling downward momentum over the past three weeks (from -0.007 on Dec 12 down to -0.021 on Jan 2). The velocity of this decline is accelerating week-by-week (-0.003, -0.005, -0.006). Given the very high weekly autocorrelation and smooth cyclical behavior of the NFCI index, a sudden upward jump that breaks this trend and pushes the value above the Jan 4 baseline by Jan 11 is highly unlikely. For short-term predictions, the descending trend strongly suppresses the likelihood of an increase. Moving further out to Feb 3 (1 month) and April 4 (3 months), the likelihood of a trend reversal or mean reversion increases significantly. Historically, cycles in this index take ~4 to 8 months to fully transition from peak to trough. Therefore, long-range uncertainty correctly increases heavily, pushing the distant estimates steadily back toward the 0.50 range matching the initial base rate anchor.
